title: Retail investors bet on these 10 small-cap stocks; they rally up to 185% in 3 months
description: Retail investors placed their bets on small-cap stocks last March '26 quarter—and the market rewarded many of them in a big way. Shareholding data shows that retail investors increased their stake in nearly 195 stocks in the Nifty Smallcap 500 Index compared with the previous December '25 quarter. (Note: Retail investors refer to resident individuals holding nominal share capital of up to Rs 2 lakh.)The move proved rewarding, with more than half of these companies delivering strong returns. Around 100 small-cap stocks rallied between 25% and 185% in just over three months, from the start of April to date. Among the biggest winners, 10 stocks skyrocketed 80% to 185%, while four emerged as multibaggers, more than doubling investors' wealth in a little over three months. (Data Source: ACE Equity) Smallcap Rally

title: SBI Funds IPO likely to open in the week of July 13, targets valuation of up to ₹1.2 lakh crore
description: SBI Funds Management is preparing to launch one of India's biggest IPOs this year, with the issue expected to open in the week beginning July 13 and raise as much as $1.2 billion through a pure offer-for-sale. The country's largest asset manager is targeting a valuation of ₹1.15 lakh crore-₹1.2 lakh crore, lower than earlier expectations, as it gauges investor sentiment amid a revival in the primary market. The listing comes ahead of several anticipated billion-dollar IPOs, including those of Jio Platforms, the National Stock Exchange and Manipal Health Enterprises. The proposed share sale will see promoters State Bank of India and France's Amundi pare their stakes, while a consortium of nine investment banks, including Kotak Mahindra Capital, Axis Capital, SBI Capital Markets, JM Financial and HSBC, has been appointed to manage the offering.

title: Middle Income Economy by World Bank – Outlook Business
description: Sri Lanka has been upgraded to an upper-middle-income economy by the World Bank after a 5% GDP expansion in 2025, marking a strong recovery from its 2022 economic crisis. Learn how tourism, financial services, IMF-backed reforms and debt restructuring helped the island nation regain its status and signal renewed economic resilience.
content:

description: Pune (Maharashtra) [India], June 29: Bajaj Finance has strengthened its personal loan offering by providing customers with a repayment tenure of up to 108 months.
